engine.name: UIBuilder Base Runtime
notes: This file is the authoritative authoring contract for the base runtime.
Use native SwiftUI-like composition. Do not invent unsupported node types.
Prefer semantic nodes over manual primitive composition wherever possible.
The renderer owns structural wrapping (VStack inside scroll/tab, toolbar lifting in navigationstack).
JSON authors own the content tree, props, state bindings, and event wiring.
Styling and layout props are applied through NodeStyle.apply(). Node-specific behavior is in UIRenderer.
Children are rendered recursively.
The renderer must not invent subtree structure beyond what is documented here.
scroll wraps children in a vertical VStack internally by default.
Use axis='horizontal' prop to switch to horizontal scroll.
JSON authors should NOT manually add a VStack wrapper inside scroll — the renderer provides it.
Container that applies explicit frame constraints and alignment to children.
Rendered as ZStack with the specified alignment.
Use alignment, idealWidth, idealHeight, fixedSize, clip, clipShape props.
Common style props (backgroundColor, cornerRadius, etc.) are also supported.
icon renders an SF Symbol as a small inline decorative glyph.
For larger standalone images, prefer the image node.
Semantic image node. Use instead of icon for full images.
systemName: SF Symbol name (mutually exclusive with name).
name: asset catalog image name (mutually exclusive with systemName).
contentMode: 'fit' (default) or 'fill'.
image is resizable by default. Use width/height props to constrain size.
Semantic node combining icon + text in one unit.
icon must be a valid SF Symbol name.
Use instead of manually composing hstack with icon and text.
Single-line text input bound to a state key.
Use secure=true for password fields (renders SecureField).
prefixIcon and suffixIcon accept SF Symbol names.
maxLength caps the string length at the binding level.
keyboardType, returnKeyType, autocapitalization accept only documented enum values.
Continuous or stepped slider bound to a state key.
step makes the slider discrete. Omit for continuous.
accentColor tints the slider track and thumb.
thumbColor, trackColorActive, trackColorInactive are declared but have limited SwiftUI support on iOS.
Geometric shape node. type defaults to 'rectangle'.
fill and strokeColor accept hex color strings.
Children are rendered on top of the shape via ZStack.
Use as decorative background, icon container, or progress ring.
Author button with props.label (string) and optional props.icon (SF Symbol name).
The renderer builds the SwiftUI Label automatically from these props.
Do not compose button label manually using children — use props.label + props.icon.
Composite node. Children are menu items (buttons or other interactive nodes).
A child with id='label' or type='label' will be used as the menu trigger label.
If no label child is provided, props title and icon are used to build the trigger.
Renderer must not auto-create label from props if a label child is present.
Semantic grouping of related content with optional header and footer.
collapsible=true renders as DisclosureGroup; isExpanded is bound to an auto-created state key.
style: 'plain' (default) renders as VStack; 'card' adds background/cornerRadius; 'inset' adds horizontal padding.
footer text is shown below children in non-collapsible mode.
Semantic TabView. Children must be of type 'tab'.
selection prop is a string key bound to state for programmatic tab switching.
Tab identifiers (id) are used as selection tags when selection is active.
The renderer owns the tab item wrapping — JSON authors should not add extra VStack inside tab.
Must be a direct child of tabview.
title and icon props define the .tabItem label.
icon must be a valid SF Symbol name.
children define the content rendered inside the tab.
The renderer wraps tab content in a VStack — do not add an outer VStack unless needed for spacing.
Owns the navigation context. Use as the root container for navigable screens.
title prop sets the navigation bar title.
toolbaritem children are automatically lifted into the navigation toolbar by the renderer.
All other children are rendered as vertical content inside the navigation stack.
JSON authors must NOT manually apply .navigationTitle or toolbar — the renderer handles this.
Must be a direct child of navigationstack to function as a toolbar item.
placement: 'leading', 'trailing' (default), or 'bottom'.
Children define the visual content of the toolbar item (typically a button or label).
When used outside navigationstack, children are rendered as a plain inline view.
Vertical document-style list container, matching SwiftUI List semantics.
Use for rows of data or settings-style content.
section children group rows naturally inside a list.
The renderer handles all List styling — do not wrap list children in additional stacks unless needed.
Groups rows inside a list with an optional header.
title prop renders as the section header text.
Fits naturally inside list. Can also be used inside other container types.
section header is renderer-owned when title prop is provided.
Table container rendered as a plain List with optional column header row.
columns: comma-separated column title strings shown in the header row.
headerVisible: true (default) shows the column header; false hides it.
alternatingRows: true alternates row background colors.
selectionMode: 'none' (default), 'single' (tap stores child id in state), 'multiple' (not yet implemented).
rowHeight: minimum row height in points.
Children of type 'section' group rows; other children are direct rows.
sortable is declared for forward compatibility but not yet implemented in the runtime.

nodeEventFormat.description: Node event values must be string event names.

topLevelEventsFormat.description: All actions live in top-level events map.

notes: Do not generate these node types for base runtime documents.

rendererOwns: VStack wrapping inside scroll and tab
navigation bar title binding from navigationstack.title prop
toolbar item placement from toolbaritem children inside navigationstack
tab item label from tab.title and tab.icon props
table column header row from table.columns prop
group card/inset visual styling from group.style prop

generationRules: Use only supported node types.
Author button using props.label (required) and props.icon (optional SF Symbol) — do not use children for button label.
Do not manually compose hstack + icon + text as a button label — use label node or button props.
Do not add a VStack wrapper inside scroll — the renderer provides it.
Do not add a VStack wrapper inside tab — the renderer provides it.
Use navigationstack as the root container for navigable screens.
Place toolbaritem nodes as direct children of navigationstack.
Use list for vertical document-style row content.
Use section inside list to group rows with optional headers.
Use image for standalone image content; use icon for inline decorative glyphs.
Do not inline actions inside node.event — use top-level events map.
Do not invent aliases for action fields.
Do not use unsupported dynamic text syntaxes.
Prefer local props over artificial wrapper nodes.
textfield requires bind prop; slider requires bind, min, and max props.
Use frame node to apply explicit alignment and ideal/fixed sizing to a subtree.
Use shape node for decorative geometric elements (backgrounds, dividers, indicators).
Use group node for semantic content sections with title/footer; prefer collapsible for accordions.
Use table for tabular data with columns; use section inside table to group rows.
Prop enum values must match the documented propValues — use exact string values only.

image.description: Standalone image — SF Symbol or asset catalog image, resizable with size constraints.

button_semantic.description: Preferred button authoring: semantic label and icon via props.

frame.description: Frame container applying alignment and ideal sizing to children.

textfield.description: Text input bound to a state key with keyboard and prefix icon.

textfield_secure.description: Secure password field with max length.

slider.description: Continuous slider bound to a state key.

shape_circle.description: Filled circle shape with a stroke.

shape_rounded_with_children.description: Rounded rectangle as a card background containing an icon.

group_plain.description: Plain group with title and footer.

table.description: Table with column headers and alternating row colors.

navigationstack.description: NavigationStack with title and toolbar buttons via toolbaritem children.

section.description: Section with optional header, fits naturally inside list.

toolbaritem.description: toolbaritem as a direct child of navigationstack — defines a navigation bar button.

label.description: Semantic icon + text in a single node.

menu.description: Contextual menu with trigger label and items from children.

tabview.description: TabView with two tabs. selection is bound to state.
